stem:
A study of 600 randomly selected commuters recorded how often each commuter rode a bicycle and whether each commuter lived within 5 miles of work. The table summarizes the data.

Bicycles oftenDoes not bicycle oftenTotal
Lives within 5 miles90110200
Lives more than 5 miles away30370400
Total120480600

If one of the 600 commuters surveyed is selected at random, what is the probability that the commuter bicycles often?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The probability is the number of commuters who bicycle often divided by the total: 120/600.

Choice B is incorrect; this is the probability of living within 5 miles. Choice C is incorrect; this is the probability of living more than 5 miles away. Choice D is incorrect; this is the probability of not bicycling often.
